mics, (8) Politics, (9) Physiognomy, (10) Engineering, (11) Botany,
(12) Industries, (13) Zoology, (14) Erotics, (15) Gemology,
(16) Hygiene, (17) Augurus and (18) Stellar lore. In the first
section which consists of eleven chapters from III to XIII and XVII
and XVIII the author describes the movements of planets both from
the astronomical and astrological points of view. He mentions
Purva Sastra, Eke, Tajjnah, Bahunam matarh which Utpala inter-
prets as the works of Vrddha Garga, Garga, Parasara, Ka^yapa,
Kasyapa, Devala, Nandin, Rsiputra, etc. In XI.I he refers to
Garga and others as the authority for Ketu's transit. This section
has many an excellent poetical piece in the descriptions of Sage
Agastya, autumn and the Seven Sages.2
In the second section he discusses the allotment of countries to
the nine regions on the basis of constellations. Similarly different
animate and inanimate objects are assigned to the jurisdiction of the
asterisms, and countries, peoples, etc.
